mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals immediate labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering rapid risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal fails to predict direction with > 52% accuracy over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with > 50% accuracy, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over individual series.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index reflects supply-side bottlenecks and manufacturing contraction, which compresses profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during inflationary supply shocks are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index breaches the 255 threshold, it signals entrenched inflation that forces aggressive monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL crosses above 255, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge over a simple trend-following benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a robust labor market expansion, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day returns with > 51% accuracy, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue generation, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show a Rank IC below 0.01 over 3-year rolling windows, or 20-day forward returns are uncorrelated with GDP revisions.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index crosses above the 110 threshold, it signals a manufacturing recovery and easing supply constraints, which improves profit outlooks and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers negative 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where INDPRO crosses above 110, or the threshold signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold strategy over a 5-year sample.
